instructions: Review classification.existing_matches and classification.novel_categories. Edit, add, or remove entries, then re-run with --resume (without --classify-only) to apply.

policy.clause: The provider of a general-purpose AI model must not generate or facilitate cyber intrusion, malware, phishing, or other cyber abuse; it must ensure adequate cybersecurity protections for the model and its physical infrastructure to prevent theft, misuse, or widespread consequences of malfunction.
policy.source_title: Насоки относно задълженията на доставчиците на ИИ с общо предназначение | Shaping Europe’s digital future

novel_categories.summary: Requests to generate or facilitate cyber abuse that does not specifically target intrusion, malware, phishing, or other named confidentiality attacks, including generic malicious cyber assistance such as abuse of online systems or services.
novel_categories.policy_fragment: generate or facilitate other cyber abuse

policy.clause: The provider of a general-purpose AI model must not generate or facilitate cyber intrusion, malware, phishing, or other cyber abuse; it must ensure adequate cybersecurity protections for the model and its physical infrastructure to prevent theft, misuse, or widespread consequences of malfunction.
policy.matched_segment: generate or facilitate other cyber abuse

source.name: EU AI Office
source.title: Насоки относно задълженията на доставчиците на ИИ с общо предназначение | Shaping Europe’s digital future

novel_categories.reasoning: This is a residual prohibition on generic cyber abuse not specifically captured by existing leaves like intrusion, malware, phishing, spoofing, or vulnerability probing.
